text.title: Routing Rules and Combined Workflow
text.summary: Routing logic to select the correct sub-methodology, canonical discovery ordering, and combined workflow pattern for multi-lens sprints.

rule.statement: Route research questions to sub-methodologies using these triggers: "What is the user trying to accomplish?" → JTBD; "Who is the user?" → Persona Building; "Does this problem really exist and matter?" → Problem Validation; "Where do users express frustration online?" → Pain Point Mining (pain-point-research).
rule.rationale: Each methodology has a different epistemic function; conflating them produces outputs that are shallower than any single methodology run thoroughly. Explicit routing prevents researchers from defaulting to their most familiar methodology regardless of the question.

rule.statement: For new product discovery, run methodologies in this order: Pain Mining → Problem Validation → JTBD → Persona Building. Do not run Persona Building before Problem Validation — personas become anchors that bias problem framing.
rule.rationale: Pain mining is inductive (discovers unknown problems); problem validation is deductive (tests a specific hypothesis); JTBD explains motivation; persona contextualizes it. Each step requires the previous step's output as input.

example.note: Building personas before problem validation: the team creates "Startup Sarah" and "Enterprise Eric," then validates only problems that fit those personas. Real problems outside the persona archetypes are systematically missed.

rule.statement: Each methodology step must produce a structured artifact before the agent proceeds: Pain Mining → pain log; Problem Validation → validation report with PROCEED/PIVOT/KILL; JTBD → job statement; Persona Building → persona card. Label each artifact with the methodology name.
rule.rationale: Agent-produced free-form summaries conflate methodology outputs; structured artifacts allow cross-methodology contradiction detection, which is the most valuable signal in a multi-lens sprint.

rule.statement: Review artifacts across methodologies for contradictions before making any product decision; contradictions are the most valuable signal.
rule.rationale: Pain mining surfaces negatives; JTBD surfaces aspirations. When these contradict (e.g., users complain about speed but their job statement shows the real bottleneck is uncertainty), the contradiction reveals the actual problem to solve.

rule.statement: Agents prompted from this combined router file produce shallower outputs than agents prompted from a dedicated methodology file; load the specific sub-methodology's content files for full guidance.
rule.rationale: Each sub-methodology has 2-3 content files and multiple templates with full detail; the router provides only summaries. An agent prompted only from the router will miss the scoring formulas, antipatterns, and tool integrations in the sub-methodology files.

text.reference: LLM prompt to identify the correct methodology
